verdict_note: Fixed for the reported Chrome+permission-allowed case (dominant cause: insecure origin). Degrades to JARVIS_VOICE_PARTIAL_BROWSER_LIMIT on genuinely unsupported browsers or OS/browser-level mic blocks outside page control.
symptom: Chrome shows 'Voice input error: not-allowed' on SpeechRecognition.start() even though site microphone permission is Allow.
root_causes: Insecure origin: Web Speech recognition only runs on a secure context (HTTPS or localhost/127.0.0.1); served over plain HTTP on a LAN name/IP Chrome blocks with not-allowed regardless of site permission.
Browser-wide or OS microphone block / device held by another app, surfacing as not-allowed or service-not-allowed.
start() race: calling start() before the prior session ended threw InvalidStateError, previously swallowed silently.

changes: Secure-context guard using window.isSecureContext + loopback host detection, with precise insecure-origin explanation.
Permissions API hint via navigator.permissions.query({name:'microphone'}) (state read only, NO audio capture), with live onchange updates.
Separated error mapping for not-allowed/service-not-allowed, no-speech, aborted, audio-capture, network.
start() race guard surfacing a calm retry hint instead of swallowing InvalidStateError.
Graceful fallback to typing on every failure path.
fix.reason_no_getusermedia: SpeechRecognition does not need getUserMedia; the read-only console test suite forbids raw-capture APIs (getUserMedia, MediaRecorder, AudioContext, navigator.mediaDevices) so audio can never be recorded/stored.

test_results.invocation_note: Run from tests/ with PYTHONPATH=.. and --noconftest to avoid a pre-existing corrupt repo-root directory (on-disk name 'hydra ' = private-use char + trailing space) that breaks pytest root enumeration; unrelated to this change.

commit_note: Awaiting approval per bundle commit policy.
